design_philosophy.core_loop: Shoot enemies, collect bell power-ups from clouds, manage dual weapon systems (air guns/ground bombs), navigate vertical scrolling stages while maintaining ship integrity
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward system around power-up collection: continuing to shoot bells changes their color/effect, but leaves player vulnerable; losing arms restricts bomb functionality until ambulance arrives
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Cute anthropomorphic spacecraft combat with cooperative multiplayer aerial combat and strategic power-up synergies
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Distinctive arm-damage system (bullets striking sides disable bombs rather than instant death), dynamic power-up cycling mechanic (shooting bells to change effects), and emphasis on cooperative two-player synchronized attacks. Pioneered 'cute 'em up' aesthetic for shooters.

difficulty.lives_system: Single bullet hit causes life loss; side hits only damage arms (functional penalty rather than death). Second arm loss = no ambulance repair available
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through power-up management: player chooses whether to continue shooting bells for color upgrades vs. securing points. Power-up loss upon death is a significant progression impediment per reviewer analysis. Cooperative attacks provide skill-based difficulty mitigation.

progression.upgrade_system: Bell-based temporary power-ups: yellow (points), white (twin cannon), blue (speed up to 5 levels), green (ship duplication/copies), red (barrier). Three-way gun alternative. Star power-up eliminates on-screen enemies. Power-ups reset on death.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Bells hidden in floating clouds; revealed by shooting clouds. Power-up effects unlocked by continuing to shoot bells after appearance.
replayability_hooks: Two-player cooperative mechanics with synchronized vertical/horizontal alignment for amplified attacks
Multiple power-up configurations and combination strategies
High difficulty encourages skill mastery and pattern learning

world_building_techniques: Anthropomorphic ship characters (TwinBee, WinBee) establish personality through gameplay mechanics
Ambulance NPC provides world-building detail (repair service within game world)

danger_signals: Arm damage system: functional degradation without full loss encourages cautious play
Power-up loss on death creates progression friction that tests player commitment
environmental_storytelling: Floating clouds as power-up containers establish hidden reward exploration
Ground enemies as distinct threat class (bomb-targeted) vs. air enemies (gun-targeted)
